task: Create a structured review plan for the target website. Fetch the homepage and identify key pages and areas to audit. Organize into categories: UX/Design, Performance, Security, SEO, Accessibility, and Content Quality. Output a prioritized checklist.

Audit the website for UX/UI quality. Analyze visual hierarchy, navigation flow, responsive design indicators, component consistency, typography, color usage, call-to-action effectiveness, and overall user experience. Provide severity-rated findings (P0-P3).

Perform a security review and vulnerability assessment. Check HTTPS configuration, security headers (CSP, HSTS), cookie security, form handling, third-party script risks, and compliance. Provide P0-P3 severity ratings with remediation steps.

Research competitor websites and gather data on industry best practices. Scrape competitor product pages, analyze their SEO strategies, and compare feature sets. Provide a competitive analysis report.
Implement quick-win code fixes: refactor the navigation component, fix accessibility bugs, add missing ARIA labels, and write unit tests for the updated components.
Synthesize ALL prior specialist findings into a comprehensive executive report. Include overall site health score, critical issues, quick wins, and a prioritized roadmap with effort estimates.
